channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: African Leadership Academy (ALA) Class of 2027 Applications Open | Scholarships & Financial Aid Available
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications are now open for the African Leadership Academy (ALA) Class of 2027, offering outstanding young Africans the opportunity to join one of the continent’s most prestigious leadership and academic institutions. Successful applicants will study in ALA’s renowned two-year diploma program while accessing generous financial aid, with 97% of students receiving scholarships or other financial […]

The post African Leadership Academy (ALA) Class of 2027 Applications Open | Scholarships & Financial Aid Available appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the African Leadership Academy (ALA) Class of 2027, offering outstanding young Africans the opportunity to join one of the continent’s most prestigious leadership and academic institutions. Successful applicants will study in ALA’s renowned two-year diploma program while accessing generous financial aid, with 97% of students receiving scholarships or other financial assistance.

Applicants must have been born on or after 1 September 2007 to be eligible for admission. The program combines world-class academics with leadership development, preparing students to tackle Africa’s most pressing social, economic, and political challenges. Annual tuition is valued at US$30,900, covering tuition, accommodation, meals, and uniforms, with financial support awarded according to each family’s demonstrated financial need.

Key Details

  • Institution: African Leadership Academy (ALA)
  • Program: Two-Year Diploma Program
  • Application Opens: 15 July 2026
  • Early Decision Deadline: 15 October 2026
  • Regular Application Deadline: 15 January 2027
  • Eligible Applicants: Students born on or after 1 September 2007
  • Financial Aid: Available to all admitted students based on financial need
  • Scholarship Coverage: Approximately 97% of students receive financial assistance
  • Annual Tuition Value: US$30,900 (includes tuition, accommodation, meals, and uniforms)
  • Location: Johannesburg, South Africa
  • Target Applicants: Outstanding young African leaders with academic excellence, leadership potential, and a passion for creating positive change.

About African Leadership Academy

African Leadership Academy is dedicated to identifying and developing Africa’s most promising young leaders. Rather than focusing solely on academic excellence, ALA seeks students who demonstrate initiative, resilience, leadership potential, and a genuine desire to create positive change within their communities and across the continent.

Students become part of a diverse pan-African community where they collaborate with peers from different cultures, backgrounds, and experiences while preparing for admission to leading universities worldwide.

Who Should Apply?

ALA is looking for young Africans who are:

  • High-performing students with strong academic potential
  • Passionate about solving problems in their communities
  • Courageous, resilient, and willing to embrace challenges
  • Curious, innovative, and eager to learn
  • Committed to making a lasting impact across Africa
  • Excited to collaborate with talented peers from across the continent

For admission into the Class of 2027, applicants must have been born on or after 1 September 2007.

African Leadership Academy Selection Criteria

ALA evaluates applicants using a holistic admissions process built around four key leadership qualities.

1. Intellectual Readiness

Successful applicants demonstrate:

  • Strong academic performance
  • Critical thinking and problem-solving skills
  • Intellectual curiosity
  • Passion for lifelong learning
  • Ability to thrive in a challenging academic environment

2. Courage and Perseverance

Applicants should show:

  • Initiative to pursue ambitious goals
  • Resilience when facing setbacks
  • Determination to overcome obstacles
  • Self-motivation and personal responsibility

3. Ownership

ALA seeks students who:

  • Dream big and set ambitious goals
  • Demonstrate leadership in their communities
  • Want to create meaningful social impact
  • Are committed to Africa’s future development

4. Interdependence

Strong candidates also:

  • Value teamwork and collaboration
  • Appreciate diverse perspectives
  • Build meaningful relationships
  • Seek support when needed
  • Practice humility and gratitude

African Leadership Academy Financial Aid

Making quality education accessible remains central to ALA’s mission.

Key financial aid facts include:

  • 97% of students receive financial assistance
  • Scholarships are available regardless of nationality
  • Awards are determined after admission decisions
  • Financial support is based on demonstrated family need
  • Tuition covers accommodation, meals, uniforms, and academic fees

Families contribute according to their financial ability, ensuring deserving students can attend regardless of economic background.

African Leadership Academy 2027 Application Timeline

Applicants should take note of the following important dates:

  • Applications Open: 15 July 2026
  • Early Decision Deadline: 15 October 2026
  • Regular Decision Deadline: 15 January 2027
  • Early Decision Results: January 2027
  • Regular Decision Results: April 2027

Submitting an Early Decision application allows applicants to receive their admissions decision sooner.

Nominate an Outstanding Young Leader

ALA also welcomes nominations from teachers, parents, mentors, and community members.

NORMINATE SOMEONE HERE

If you know a young African with exceptional leadership potential, you can nominate them to encourage their application. The academy reviews nominations and contacts selected students, inviting them to apply for admission.

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

African Leadership Academy maintains a non-discrimination admissions policy and welcomes qualified applicants from all backgrounds. Admission decisions are based solely on applicants’ abilities, leadership potential, and overall suitability for the program, regardless of gender, race, religion, disability, nationality, ethnicity, wealth, or income.

Why Apply to African Leadership Academy?

Studying at African Leadership Academy provides students with:

  • A world-class leadership-focused education
  • Access to generous scholarships and financial aid
  • A diverse pan-African learning community
  • Intensive leadership development
  • Preparation for leading universities worldwide
  • Lifelong membership in the ALA global network
  • Opportunities to address Africa’s biggest challenges through innovation and service

Young Africans with bold ideas, strong leadership potential, and a passion for creating positive change are encouraged to apply before the application deadlines.

Application Deadline: 15 January 2027 (Regular Decision)

Financial Aid: Available to all admitted students based on demonstrated financial need.

APPLY NOW 

The post African Leadership Academy (ALA) Class of 2027 Applications Open | Scholarships & Financial Aid Available appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: EAC Student Mobility Scholarship 2026/2027: 197 Fully Funded Undergraduate, Master’s & PhD Scholarships Across East Africa
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Inter-University Council for East Africa (IUCEA) has opened applications for the East African Community (EAC) Student Mobility Scholarship Scheme (EAC-SMS) 2026/2027, offering 197 scholarships for Diploma, Undergraduate, Master’s and PhD studies at 28 universities across East Africa. The scholarship enables students from eligible East African Community countries to study in another EAC Partner State […]

The post EAC Student Mobility Scholarship 2026/2027: 197 Fully Funded Undergraduate, Master’s & PhD Scholarships Across East Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Inter-University Council for East Africa (IUCEA) has opened applications for the East African Community (EAC) Student Mobility Scholarship Scheme (EAC-SMS) 2026/2027, offering 197 scholarships for Diploma, Undergraduate, Master’s and PhD studies at 28 universities across East Africa.

The scholarship enables students from eligible East African Community countries to study in another EAC Partner State while receiving financial support for tuition, travel, health insurance and research costs. Applications close on 20 August 2026.

Key Details

Scholarship EAC Student Mobility Scholarship Scheme (EAC-SMS) 2026/2027
Host Organisation Inter-University Council for East Africa (IUCEA)
Study Levels Diploma, Undergraduate, Master’s, PhD
Number of Scholarships 197
Participating Universities 28 Universities
Host Countries Burundi, Kenya, Rwanda, South Sudan, Tanzania & Uganda
Eligible Applicants Nationals and residents of EAC Partner States
Scholarship Benefits Tuition waiver, examination fees, travel, health insurance, research support (Master’s & PhD)
Application Fee None
Deadline 20 August 2026

What is the EAC Student Mobility Scholarship Scheme (EAC-SMS)?

The East African Community Student Mobility Scholarship Scheme (EAC-SMS) is a regional initiative coordinated by the Inter-University Council for East Africa (IUCEA). The programme supports student mobility by providing scholarships for learners to study in an EAC Partner State other than their own, strengthening collaboration among universities and enhancing regional integration through higher education.

For the 2026/2027 academic year, the programme offers scholarships at 28 universities across Burundi, Kenya, Rwanda, South Sudan, Tanzania, and Uganda.

EAC Student Mobility Scholarship Benefits

The scholarship operates as a cost-sharing programme, ensuring students receive significant financial support throughout their studies.

Host Universities Cover

  • 100% Tuition fees
  • Examination fees

IUCEA Covers

  • International travel expenses
  • Health insurance
  • Research support for Master’s and PhD students

Students/Parents Cover

  • Accommodation
  • Daily living expenses
  • Other university-related costs not covered by the scholarship

Number of Scholarships Available

A total of 197 scholarships are available for the 2026/2027 intake, distributed as follows:

Study Level Scholarships
Undergraduate 95
Diploma 1
Master’s 90
PhD 11
Total 197

These opportunities span 164 academic programmes offered by 28 universities across six East African countries.

Participating Countries

Scholarships are available at universities in:

  • Burundi
  • Kenya
  • Rwanda
  • South Sudan
  • Tanzania
  • Uganda

Students must study in a country different from their home country within the East African Community.

Available Degree Programmes

Applicants can choose from a diverse range of academic disciplines, including:

  • Computer Science
  • Information Technology
  • Cyber Security
  • Statistics
  • Medicine
  • Nursing
  • Public Health
  • Renewable Energy
  • Agriculture
  • Environmental Science
  • Business Administration
  • Economics
  • Finance
  • Engineering
  • Biotechnology
  • Climate Change
  • Education
  • Social Work
  • International Studies
  • Peace and Conflict Studies
  • Tourism
  • Development Studies
  • Law
  • Entrepreneurship
  • Regional Integration
  • Project Planning and Management
  • Monitoring and Evaluation
  • Health Informatics
  • Food Security
  • Sustainable Agriculture
  • Astronomy
  • Swahili Studies

In total, applicants can select from 164 programmes hosted by universities across the East African Community.

Who Can Apply?

To qualify for the EAC Student Mobility Scholarship 2026/2027, applicants must:

  • Be a national and resident of an EAC Partner State.
  • Apply to study in another EAC Partner State (not their own).
  • Meet the admission requirements of the chosen university.
  • Demonstrate excellent academic performance.
  • Hold at least an Upper Second-Class Honours degree (or equivalent) for Master’s and PhD programmes.
  • Be below 35 years of age for Master’s programmes.
  • Be below 45 years of age for PhD programmes.
  • Relevant work experience is an added advantage for PhD applicants.

APPLY NOW

Eligible countries include:

  • Burundi
  • Kenya
  • Rwanda
  • South Sudan
  • Tanzania
  • Uganda

Required Application Documents

Applicants should prepare the following documents:

  • Motivation letter
  • Updated and signed Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Certified academic certificates and transcripts
  • University application form and supporting attachments
  • Passport-size photograph
  • Proof of nationality
  • Proof of residency
  • Evidence of leadership, awards, or community service (where applicable)
  • Letters of reference or other supporting documents (if available)

Foreign qualifications must be officially equated by the relevant national higher education authority, while documents not in English must be accompanied by certified English translations.

How to Apply for the EAC Student Mobility Scholarship

The application process consists of two key stages:

Step 1: Select Your Programme

Browse the official list of eligible universities and programmes and choose your preferred course of study. APPLY NOW

Step 2: Submit Your Scholarship Application

Complete the online IUCEA scholarship application and upload all required documents. APPLY NOW

Important: Applicants should not apply directly to universities or pay university application fees. IUCEA coordinates university admissions for successful scholarship applicants.

Important Information

  • No university application fee is required.
  • Applicants may apply to more than one participating university.
  • Only one scholarship will ultimately be awarded to each successful applicant.
  • Students already admitted to a participating university remain eligible to apply for the scholarship.

EAC Student Mobility Scholarship Deadline

Application Deadline: 20 August 2026

Late applications will not be considered. Applicants are encouraged to submit all required documents well before the deadline.

Why Apply for the EAC Student Mobility Scholarship?

The EAC Student Mobility Scholarship offers much more than financial support. Scholars benefit from:

  • Studying at leading universities across East Africa.
  • Building regional and international professional networks.
  • Gaining cross-cultural academic experiences.
  • Enhancing career prospects through internationally recognized qualifications.
  • Contributing to regional integration and sustainable development.

Apply for the EAC Student Mobility Scholarship 2026/2027 Today

If you are an ambitious student from Burundi, Kenya, Rwanda, South Sudan, Tanzania, or Uganda, this is an outstanding opportunity to pursue a fully supported undergraduate, Master’s, or PhD degree at a top university in another East African Community country.

With 197 scholarships available across 28 universities and 164 academic programmes, the EAC Student Mobility Scholarship 2026/2027 is one of the region’s most competitive and impactful higher education opportunities.

Application Deadline: 20 August 2026. Don’t miss your chance to advance your education while becoming part of a stronger, more integrated East African Community.

APPLY NOW

The post EAC Student Mobility Scholarship 2026/2027: 197 Fully Funded Undergraduate, Master’s & PhD Scholarships Across East Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GitLab Foundation Powering Economic Opportunity Fund 2026: Up to $500,000 in Flexible Grants for Workforce Development Organizations in the United States
item.dc:creator: Nana Safo
item.description:
Empowering Organizations to Create Lasting Economic Opportunities for American Workers The GitLab Foundation has officially launched the second round of its Powering Economic Opportunity Fund (PEOF), a major funding initiative designed to strengthen workforce development across the United States. Through this initiative, the Foundation is committing $4 million in flexible grant funding to support organizations […]

The post GitLab Foundation Powering Economic Opportunity Fund 2026: Up to $500,000 in Flexible Grants for Workforce Development Organizations in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Empowering Organizations to Create Lasting Economic Opportunities for American Workers

The GitLab Foundation has officially launched the second round of its Powering Economic Opportunity Fund (PEOF), a major funding initiative designed to strengthen workforce development across the United States. Through this initiative, the Foundation is committing $4 million in flexible grant funding to support organizations that partner directly with employers, unions, and industry leaders to improve job quality, expand work-based learning opportunities, and create sustainable career pathways for workers.

Rather than simply supporting training programs, the Powering Economic Opportunity Fund focuses on transforming employer practices that help workers remain employed, advance professionally, and achieve long-term income growth.

Organizations selected under this funding opportunity may receive grants of up to $500,000 over a period of one to two years.

About the Powering Economic Opportunity Fund

The Powering Economic Opportunity Fund is part of a broader two-year, $16 million partnership between the GitLab Foundation and the Families and Workers Fund.

The initiative was created to address one of today’s biggest workforce challenges: although thousands of workers receive skills training every year, many struggle to remain employed or advance into long-term careers. The Foundation believes that solving this challenge requires improving employer practices—not just worker training.

Round 2 of the program therefore places greater emphasis on employer-side innovations that improve employee retention, career progression, workplace quality, and long-term earning potential.

Why This Fund Matters

The United States is experiencing major economic transformation driven by several rapidly expanding sectors, including:

  • Clean and renewable energy
  • Advanced manufacturing
  • Infrastructure development
  • Skilled construction trades
  • Industrial technology

Demand for skilled professionals such as electricians, welders, energy technicians, construction managers, and industrial specialists continues to increase.

At the same time:

  • Many experienced workers are retiring.
  • Employers face growing shortages of skilled labor.
  • Artificial Intelligence is reshaping the labor market by reducing many entry-level knowledge jobs.
  • Workers increasingly need access to durable career opportunities offering stable wages and long-term growth.

The GitLab Foundation believes that investing in better employer practices can help bridge this gap and produce lasting economic benefits for workers.

Funding Available

Successful applicants may receive:

  • Maximum grant amount: Up to $500,000
  • Funding pool: $4 million
  • Grant duration:
    • One year
    • Two years
  • Flexible funding that allows organizations to implement innovative workforce solutions.

What the Fund Supports

The Powering Economic Opportunity Fund is specifically interested in initiatives that improve long-term employment outcomes rather than only increasing training participation.

Priority will be given to projects that strengthen relationships between employers and workforce organizations while improving worker success.

Areas of interest include:

1. Employer Partnerships

Every application must include an active partnership with at least one of the following:

  • Employer
  • Labor union
  • Industry association
  • Workforce industry partner

Strong partnerships may include:

  • Hiring commitments
  • Employer-recognized credentials
  • Curriculum co-development
  • Employer co-investment
  • Apprenticeships
  • Work-based learning opportunities

Applications that do not include an active employer or industry partnership will not move beyond the concept note stage.

2. Employer Practice Change

The Foundation seeks projects that improve workplace quality through employer-led innovations.

Examples include:

  • Structured career progression pathways
  • Better supervisory and management practices
  • Employer-funded education programs
  • Predictable work schedules
  • Childcare support initiatives
  • Employee retention strategies
  • Career advancement programs
  • Workplace policies that improve long-term earnings

3. Durable Impact

Applicants should demonstrate how successful practices will continue after grant funding ends.

Possible long-term sustainability models include:

  • Employer investment
  • Collective bargaining agreements
  • Public funding
  • Other long-term financing mechanisms

The Foundation is particularly interested in projects capable of producing lasting improvements in worker earnings over time.

Expected Impact

The GitLab Foundation prioritizes initiatives that generate measurable economic outcomes.

Its goal is to create projects capable of producing at least:

  • $100 in increased worker earnings for every $1 invested by the Foundation.

This focus ensures that funded initiatives deliver meaningful and measurable economic benefits.

Who Can Apply?

Eligible applicants include:

  • U.S.-based 501(c)(3) nonprofit organizations.
  • Organizations operating under a fiscal sponsor.
  • Nonprofit partnerships involving eligible 501(c)(3) organizations.

Organizations that are not registered as 501(c)(3) nonprofits may still apply if they partner with an eligible fiscal sponsor or nonprofit organization.

Application Process

The application process consists of multiple stages.

Step 1: Submit a Concept Note

Applicants begin by submitting a short concept note describing:

  • Their proposed initiative
  • Employer partnerships
  • Workforce impact
  • Long-term sustainability
  • Expected outcomes

Concept notes will be reviewed on a rolling basis.

Step 2: Invitation to Full Application

Selected organizations will receive invitations to submit a comprehensive application during September 2026.

Step 3: Final Review

Applications will undergo detailed review through November 2026.

Step 4: Grant Awards

Successful organizations will be announced in early December 2026.

Request for Proposal or APPLY HERE

Application Deadline

Deadline to submit the concept note: August 12, 2026.

Organizations are encouraged to apply as early as possible to allow adequate time for review.

For more information and questions, visit GITLAB Foundation HERE

Or send an email to:grants@gitlabfoundation.org

Key Features of the Fund

  • Up to $500,000 in grant funding.
  • Flexible one- or two-year grant period.
  • Focus on sustainable workforce development.
  • Strong emphasis on employer partnerships.
  • Supports innovative workplace improvements.
  • Encourages work-based learning models.
  • Promotes long-term career advancement.
  • Supports higher lifetime earnings for workers.
  • Requires sustainability beyond grant funding.
  • Backed by a $16 million partnership between GitLab Foundation and the Families and Workers Fund.

About the Families and Workers Fund

The Families and Workers Fund is a collaborative funding platform supported by more than 40 philanthropic partners working together to build a stronger and more equitable economy.

The organization manages approximately $130 million in collaborative funding and is housed at the Amalgamated Foundation, an independent 501(c)(3) charitable organization.

Its partnership with the GitLab Foundation reflects a shared commitment to expanding economic opportunity and improving workforce outcomes across the United States.

Why Organizations Should Apply

Organizations working at the intersection of workforce development and employer engagement have an opportunity to secure significant flexible funding while helping shape the future of work in America.

The fund goes beyond traditional workforce grants by investing in employer practices that improve retention, advancement, and long-term earnings. Organizations with strong employer partnerships, innovative workforce models, and sustainable financing plans will be particularly well positioned to compete.

For more Grants opportunities visit OFY Website HERE

Conclusion

The GitLab Foundation’s Powering Economic Opportunity Fund 2026 represents an important opportunity for workforce development organizations committed to creating lasting economic change. By investing in employer partnerships, innovative workplace practices, and sustainable career pathways, the Foundation aims to ensure that workers not only gain employment but also build rewarding, long-term careers with increased earning potential.

Eligible organizations are encouraged to prepare strong employer partnerships, develop sustainable workforce strategies, and submit their concept notes before the August 12, 2026 deadline to be considered for grants of up to $500,000.

The post GitLab Foundation Powering Economic Opportunity Fund 2026: Up to $500,000 in Flexible Grants for Workforce Development Organizations in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Eastern Partnership Civil Society Fellowships Without Borders 2026: Receive €5,000 to Strengthen Cross-Border Civic Leadership
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The Eastern Partnership Civil Society Facility has officially launched applications for the Fellowships Without Borders 2026, an exciting opportunity designed to empower civic leaders and activists committed to creating meaningful social change across borders. The programme supports individuals from Armenia, Azerbaijan, Belarus, Georgia, Moldova, and Ukraine who are working to strengthen civil society through international […]

The post Eastern Partnership Civil Society Fellowships Without Borders 2026: Receive €5,000 to Strengthen Cross-Border Civic Leadership appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Eastern Partnership Civil Society Facility has officially launched applications for the Fellowships Without Borders 2026, an exciting opportunity designed to empower civic leaders and activists committed to creating meaningful social change across borders.

The programme supports individuals from Armenia, Azerbaijan, Belarus, Georgia, Moldova, and Ukraine who are working to strengthen civil society through international cooperation, learning, innovation, advocacy, and community engagement.

Successful applicants will receive approximately €5,000 in financial support, mentoring, coaching, networking opportunities, and access to one of the region’s strongest civil society alumni networks.

Application Deadline: 11 September 2026

About the Eastern Partnership Civil Society Fellowship Programme

Since its launch in 2017, the Eastern Partnership Civil Society Fellowship Programme has supported more than 250 civic leaders across the Eastern Partnership region.

The programme was established to strengthen:

  • Civic leadership
  • Democratic participation
  • Community development
  • Accountability
  • Public engagement
  • Regional cooperation
  • Social innovation

Through financial support, mentoring, training, and international networking, Fellows have implemented projects that continue to improve governance and strengthen civil society across their communities.

What is “Fellowships Without Borders”?

The Fellowships Without Borders initiative recognizes that today’s civic challenges often extend beyond national boundaries.

Many activists:

  • Work with communities spread across several countries.
  • Live temporarily abroad.
  • Operate in exile.
  • Collaborate internationally.
  • Build regional partnerships.

Rather than limiting impact to one country, this Fellowship encourages applicants to use international experience and cross-border cooperation to benefit communities in the Eastern Partnership region.

Projects may be implemented in:

  • Eastern Partnership countries
  • European Union Member States
  • EU Candidate Countries

However, every project should ultimately generate positive impact for communities from the Eastern Partnership countries.

Fellowship Benefits

Selected Fellows will receive:

  • Approximately €5,000 to implement their Fellowship project.
  • Individual mentoring and expert coaching.
  • Capacity-building and leadership training.
  • Learning and professional development opportunities.
  • Invitations to international networking events.
  • Access to the Eastern Partnership Civil Society Fellowship Alumni Network.
  • Long-term collaboration opportunities with regional civic leaders.

Fellowship Streams

Applicants can apply under one of two Fellowship categories.

1. Community Engagement Fellowships

These Fellowships support projects focusing on:

  • Civic participation
  • Community engagement
  • Policy advocacy
  • Public accountability
  • Organizational development
  •  Research and evidence generation
  • Civil society cooperation
  • Community mobilization
  • Awareness campaigns

2. Civic Digital Fellowships

Designed for civic technology professionals working on:

  • Digital public participation platforms
  • Civic technology solutions
  • Transparency tools
  • Open data initiatives
  • Digital accountability systems
  • Technology for social good
  • Innovation that strengthens democratic participation

Who Can Apply?

Applicants must:

  • Be citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Belarus, Georgia, Moldova, or Ukraine.
  • Be at least 18 years old.
  • Have a proven record of civic engagement or civic innovation.
  • Demonstrate English language proficiency (minimum B1).
  • Not have previously participated in the Eastern Partnership Civil Society Fellowship Programme.

Please note that Fellowships are awarded to individuals, not organizations.

What Makes a “Without Borders” Fellowship Project?

Applicants must demonstrate:

1. Commitment to Positive Social Change

Projects should clearly contribute to improving communities through civic engagement, leadership, or advocacy.

2. Cross-Border Component

Applicants should explain one or both of the following:

Learning Across Borders

Show how learning activities in another country will strengthen civic engagement or policy work within their community.

Supporting Communities Abroad

Demonstrate how they will support fellow citizens and civil society actors located in another country while contributing to advocacy, policy dialogue, or community development.

Eligible Activities

Supported activities include:

  • Study visits
  • Job shadowing
  • Internships
  • Research projects
  • Advocacy campaigns
  • Community mobilization
  • Organizational development
  • Mentorship programmes
  • Capacity-building initiatives
  • Cross-border partnerships
  • Civic technology development
  • Data collection and analysis
  • Evidence-based policy initiatives

Projects should produce practical, measurable outcomes while strengthening the Fellow’s leadership capacity.

Priority Areas

Projects should align with one or more of the programme’s thematic priorities:

  • Resilient and sustainable economies
  • Rule of law and accountability
  • Security
  • Climate and environmental resilience
  • Inclusive digital transformation
  • Participatory governance
  • Social justice

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  • Online application form (in English)
  • Fellowship project proposal
  • Budget
  • Activity plan
  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Recommendation letter
  • Proof of citizenship
  • Proof of English language proficiency

Applicants who cannot safely use the online application system may contact the programme team for alternative arrangements.

Information Webinar

Applicants are encouraged to participate in the programme’s information webinar.

Date: 15 July 2026

The webinar explains:

  • Eligibility requirements
  • Fellowship expectations
  • Tips for preparing a competitive application
  • Common mistakes to avoid

A recording will also be made available after the live session.

Why You Should Apply

The Fellowship offers far more than financial support.

Participants will gain:

  • International exposure
  • Leadership development
  • Professional mentoring
  • Access to regional experts
  • Strong cross-border partnerships
  • Membership in a growing alumni network
  • Opportunities to influence policy and strengthen democratic participation

It is particularly valuable for civic leaders seeking to expand their impact beyond national borders while maintaining strong connections to their communities.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 11 September 2026

Applications submitted after the deadline may not be considered.

Apply Here

Final Thoughts

The Eastern Partnership Civil Society Fellowships Without Borders 2026 provide an outstanding opportunity for emerging civic leaders to transform local ideas into regional impact.

Whether you are a community organizer, policy advocate, civic tech innovator, researcher, or grassroots activist, this Fellowship equips you with the financial resources, mentoring, and international partnerships needed to strengthen democratic participation and build lasting social change across the Eastern Partnership region.

For more information on this fellowship opportunity, visit here 

Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY, click here

The post Eastern Partnership Civil Society Fellowships Without Borders 2026: Receive €5,000 to Strengthen Cross-Border Civic Leadership appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: CIVICUS External Learning and Community Design Consultancy 2026: Apply for a Global Consultancy to Build the WeRise Global Community
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
CIVICUS Global Alliance is inviting experienced consultants and consulting teams to submit Expressions of Interest (EOIs) for the External Learning and Community Design Consultancy 2026. This three-month consultancy presents an exciting opportunity for professionals specializing in community building, participatory design, organizational learning, and movement strengthening to help shape the future of the WeRise Global FoPA […]

The post CIVICUS External Learning and Community Design Consultancy 2026: Apply for a Global Consultancy to Build the WeRise Global Community appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
CIVICUS Global Alliance is inviting experienced consultants and consulting teams to submit Expressions of Interest (EOIs) for the External Learning and Community Design Consultancy 2026. This three-month consultancy presents an exciting opportunity for professionals specializing in community building, participatory design, organizational learning, and movement strengthening to help shape the future of the WeRise Global FoPA Solidarity Infrastructure.

The consultancy aims to transform an existing network of grassroots organizations into a sustainable, movement-owned global community that strengthens collaboration, learning, solidarity, and collective action beyond traditional funding relationships.

Application Deadline: 5 August 2026

About CIVICUS

CIVICUS is a global alliance dedicated to defending people power and strengthening civil society worldwide. With more than 17,000 members across over 180 countries, the organization works to:

  • Protect civic freedoms.
  • Strengthen civil society organizations.
  • Promote accountability.
  • Support grassroots movements.
  • Amplify voices from the Global South.
  • Build stronger global solidarity among civil society actors.

Through research, advocacy, partnerships, and capacity-building, CIVICUS works to ensure that citizens can organize freely and participate meaningfully in shaping their societies.

About the WeRise Initiative

Grassroots organizations worldwide continue to defend the right to peaceful assembly despite:

  • Shrinking civic spaces.
  • Increasing political repression.
  • Complex operating environments.
  • Funding limitations.
  • Fragmented support systems.

Through two successful WeRise cohorts, CIVICUS has already supported:

  • 32 grassroots organizations
  • Movement collectives across multiple regions
  • Learning exchanges
  • Innovation spaces
  • Grant support
  • Solidarity initiatives

As the current funding cycle concludes, CIVICUS aims to preserve the relationships, trust, and knowledge developed through the programme by creating the WeRise Global FoPA Solidarity Infrastructure.

Rather than remaining a donor-supported network, the vision is to establish a sustainable, movement-led community that promotes:

  • Cross-border collaboration
  • Shared learning
  • Mutual support
  • Collective advocacy
  • Long-term solidarity

Purpose of the Consultancy

The selected consultant will lead a participatory process that enables members of the WeRise network to co-create the future structure of the community.

The consultancy seeks to ensure that the future community is:

  • Member-owned
  • Sustainable
  • Inclusive
  • Collaborative
  • Built around the priorities of grassroots movements

Consultancy Objectives

The consultant will work closely with CIVICUS and community members to:

  1. Assess existing relationships, strengths, learning needs, and expectations across the WeRise network.
  2. Co-design the future vision and identity of the community.
  3. Develop governance structures and engagement models.
  4. Recommend sustainable collaboration mechanisms.
  5. Design a long-term solidarity infrastructure.
  6. Produce a practical roadmap for launching the WeRise Global Community.

Scope of Work

Successful consultants will undertake several key activities.

1. Community Discovery and Ecosystem Assessment

The consultant will:

  • Conduct participatory assessments.
  • Identify community strengths.
  • Map relationships.
  • Explore collaboration opportunities.
  • Assess members’ aspirations and priorities.

2. Community Listening Process

The consultant will design and facilitate an inclusive consultation process that:

  • Encourages broad participation.
  • Builds trust.
  • Ensures equitable representation.
  • Captures diverse perspectives.
  • Identifies priorities for the future community.

3. Co-create the Community Model

Working collaboratively with members, the consultant will help define:

  • Community purpose
  • Vision
  • Identity
  • Guiding principles
  • Governance model
  • Ways of working
  • Member ownership mechanisms

4. Develop the Community Value Proposition

The consultancy will identify the services and opportunities the community should provide, including:

  • Peer learning
  • Collaboration opportunities
  • Knowledge exchange
  • Solidarity initiatives
  • Capacity strengthening
  • Collective advocacy

5. Community Engagement Framework

The consultant will recommend systems for sustaining active member participation through:

  • Leadership development
  • Community governance
  • Learning opportunities
  • Collaboration models
  • Shared stewardship

6. Implementation Roadmap

A strategic roadmap will outline:

  • Launch phases
  • Governance recommendations
  • Priority actions
  • Community development milestones
  • Long-term sustainability strategies

Expected Deliverables

The selected consultant will produce:

  • Inception Report
  • Community Assessment Report
  • WeRise Community Design Framework
  • Community Implementation Roadmap
  • Final Consultancy Report

Required Qualifications

Applicants should demonstrate experience in:

  • Movement building
  • Community organizing
  • Network facilitation
  • Participatory learning
  • Organizational learning
  • Communities of Practice
  • Social movements
  • Civic space
  • Human rights
  • Human-centered design
  • Systems thinking
  • Participatory design methodologies
  • Qualitative research
  • Report writing
  • Multi-country stakeholder engagement

Consultancy Duration

The consultancy will run for three months, from:

  • September 2026
  • October 2026
  • November 2026

Application Requirements

Interested consultants should submit:

  • A full Curriculum Vitae (CV).
  • A maximum two-page cover letter describing:
    • Relevant experience.
    • Technical expertise.
    • Proposed methodology.
    • Approach to delivering the consultancy.
  • A proposed consultancy budget.

Applications should be sent via email to FoPA@civicus.org  with the subject line:

EOI – External Learning and Community Design Consultancy

Why You Should Apply

This consultancy offers professionals an opportunity to:

  • Influence a global movement-building initiative.
  • Work alongside leading civil society organizations.
  • Design innovative community governance models.
  • Support grassroots movements worldwide.
  • Strengthen global civic participation.
  • Promote sustainable collaboration beyond donor funding.
  • Contribute to long-term systems change within international civil society.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 5 August 2026

Interested consultants are encouraged to submit complete applications before the deadline, as late submissions may not be considered.

Final Thoughts

The CIVICUS External Learning and Community Design Consultancy provides a unique opportunity for experienced facilitators, movement builders, and organizational learning experts to help shape a global community committed to defending civic freedoms and strengthening grassroots collaboration.

If you have experience in participatory design, community building, and international civil society, this consultancy allows you to contribute to a transformative initiative that will continue supporting movements long after traditional grant funding has ended.

For more information on this opportunity, visit here

Discover more global consultancy opportunities on OFY, click here 

The post CIVICUS External Learning and Community Design Consultancy 2026: Apply for a Global Consultancy to Build the WeRise Global Community appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded PhD Scholarship 2027 at the University of Melbourne: Research Accessibility and Exposure to Biodiversity Among Underprivileged Communities in Cities
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The University of Melbourne is inviting applications for an exciting Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) Research Degree Opportunity focused on Accessibility and Exposure to Biodiversity Among Underprivileged Communities in Cities. Hosted by the School of Agriculture, Food and Ecosystem Sciences within the Faculty of Science, this interdisciplinary project offers aspiring researchers the opportunity to investigate how […]

The post Fully Funded PhD Scholarship 2027 at the University of Melbourne: Research Accessibility and Exposure to Biodiversity Among Underprivileged Communities in Cities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Melbourne is inviting applications for an exciting Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) Research Degree Opportunity focused on Accessibility and Exposure to Biodiversity Among Underprivileged Communities in Cities. Hosted by the School of Agriculture, Food and Ecosystem Sciences within the Faculty of Science, this interdisciplinary project offers aspiring researchers the opportunity to investigate how biodiversity access affects the wellbeing of disadvantaged urban communities.

Students admitted to the project will automatically be considered for a range of University of Melbourne scholarships, making this an excellent opportunity for candidates seeking funded doctoral studies in Australia.

The project is expected to commence on 1 February 2027 and will be based at the University’s Burnley Campus in Melbourne, Australia.

About the PhD Project

Cities around the world continue to expand rapidly through urbanization. While urban development brings economic opportunities, it also increases the separation between people and the natural environment. As cities grow, access to biodiversity—including parks, native vegetation, wildlife habitats, and green spaces—becomes increasingly unequal.

This research project aims to understand how accessibility to biodiversity differs among underprivileged urban communities and how unequal access influences physical, mental, and social wellbeing.

Using an interdisciplinary approach that combines ecological science, geography, urban planning, spatial analysis, and social research, the project seeks to generate evidence that can support more equitable and sustainable city planning.

Research Focus

The PhD research will investigate several important questions, including:

  • How biodiversity is distributed across urban neighbourhoods.
  • Which communities have the greatest access to biodiversity.
  • How socioeconomic disadvantage affects biodiversity exposure.
  • The relationship between biodiversity access and community wellbeing.
  • Strategies that governments and planners can adopt to improve equitable access to urban biodiversity.

The research will specifically focus on Melbourne, Australia, serving as a case study with global relevance for sustainable cities.

Why Biodiversity Matters

Biodiversity offers numerous benefits that extend beyond environmental conservation.

Research has shown that access to biodiverse environments can contribute to:

  • Improved mental health
  • Reduced stress and anxiety
  • Better physical health
  • Lower disease risks
  • Enhanced environmental education
  • Stronger community wellbeing
  • Increased resilience to climate change
  • Better quality of urban life

Despite these benefits, biodiversity-rich environments are often concentrated in wealthier neighbourhoods, leaving disadvantaged communities with fewer opportunities to experience nature.

This project seeks to address these inequalities through evidence-based research.

Research Methodology

The successful PhD candidate will apply multiple research approaches, including:

Social Research

  • Community engagement
  • Surveys
  • Interviews
  • Socioeconomic analysis

Ecological Research

  • Biodiversity assessments
  • Species distribution analysis
  • Habitat evaluation

Spatial Analysis

  • Geographic Information Systems (GIS)
  • Urban mapping
  • Accessibility modelling
  • Spatial data analysis

This interdisciplinary methodology will generate practical recommendations for urban planners and policymakers.

Degree Awarded

Successful applicants will pursue a:

Doctor of Philosophy (PhD)

at the University of Melbourne.

Scholarships

Applicants are automatically considered for a range of University of Melbourne scholarships.

Some additional scholarships may require separate applications.

Scholarship opportunities may include support for:

  • Tuition fees
  • Living allowance
  • Research costs
  • Relocation assistance (where applicable)

Applicants are encouraged to review scholarship requirements on the University of Melbourne website.

Research Fields

The project aligns with several internationally recognized research disciplines, including:

  • Human Geography
  • Ecology
  • Urban and Regional Planning
  • Ecological Applications

Supervisory Team

The successful candidate will work under the supervision of leading researchers:

Principal Supervisor

  • Marie C. Dade
    • School of Agriculture, Food and Ecosystem Sciences

Internal Co-supervisor

  • Ilan Wiesel
    • School of Geography, Earth and Atmospheric Sciences

Host Institution

The research will be undertaken at:

University of Melbourne

Faculty of Science

School of Agriculture, Food and Ecosystem Sciences

Burnley Campus

Melbourne, Australia

Project Start Date

The anticipated commencement date is:

1 February 2027

Prospective students are encouraged to submit applications early to allow sufficient time for admission and scholarship processing.

Why You Should Apply

This PhD offers an outstanding opportunity to:

  • Conduct interdisciplinary research addressing global sustainability challenges.
  • Work alongside internationally recognized researchers.
  • Gain advanced expertise in ecology, urban planning, geography, and environmental science.
  • Contribute evidence that can improve biodiversity equity in cities.
  • Be automatically considered for University of Melbourne scholarships.
  • Build an academic and professional career in environmental sustainability and urban resilience.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should submit their application through the University of Melbourne Research Degree Opportunities portal.

Applicants should review:

  • PhD admission requirements
  • Scholarship eligibility
  • Research project details
  • Required supporting documents

Applications are encouraged well before the project start date to maximize scholarship opportunities.

Apply Here 

For more information on this scholarship opportunity, visit here 

Discover more scholarship opportunities on OFY, click here 

The post Fully Funded PhD Scholarship 2027 at the University of Melbourne: Research Accessibility and Exposure to Biodiversity Among Underprivileged Communities in Cities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Active Play Challenge 2026: Win Up to USD $100,000 to Create Innovative Active Play Opportunities for Children and Young People Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Active Play Challenge 2026, a global initiative inviting legally registered organizations from around the world to develop innovative solutions that make active play more accessible, inclusive, and equitable for children and young people. The challenge seeks practical projects that transform cities into healthier, more playful environments where children can […]

The post Active Play Challenge 2026: Win Up to USD $100,000 to Create Innovative Active Play Opportunities for Children and Young People Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Active Play Challenge 2026, a global initiative inviting legally registered organizations from around the world to develop innovative solutions that make active play more accessible, inclusive, and equitable for children and young people. The challenge seeks practical projects that transform cities into healthier, more playful environments where children can grow, learn, stay active, and thrive.

As urbanization continues to reshape communities worldwide, opportunities for children to engage in regular active play have steadily declined. Limited access to safe public spaces, busy lifestyles, inadequate infrastructure, and changing social environments have reduced the amount of time children spend playing outdoors and engaging in physical activity. The Active Play Challenge aims to reverse this trend by supporting organizations that are reimagining urban spaces and designing programmes that integrate active play into everyday life.

Successful initiatives will receive funding ranging from USD $50,000 to USD $100,000, together with expert technical guidance to help refine and implement their projects. Shortlisted finalists will also benefit from learning opportunities and peer-to-peer collaboration with other innovators working to improve children’s health and wellbeing globally.

If your organization is passionate about creating healthier cities, promoting childhood development, encouraging physical activity, or designing innovative public spaces, this challenge provides an outstanding opportunity to turn your ideas into lasting impact.

About the Active Play Challenge

The Active Play Challenge is a global competition focused on increasing opportunities for active play for children and young people in cities across the world.

The challenge recognizes that active play is essential for children’s:

  • Physical development
  • Mental wellbeing
  • Cognitive growth
  • Social development
  • Emotional health
  • Creativity
  • Learning
  • Independence

Despite its importance, many children today have fewer opportunities to play due to increasing urbanization, limited recreational spaces, traffic congestion, safety concerns, and changing lifestyles.

The challenge seeks innovative initiatives that help overcome these barriers and make active play an integral part of daily life.

Its vision is simple:

To make cities active through play, everywhere, every day.

Why Active Play Matters

Active play is much more than recreation.

Research consistently shows that regular active play contributes to:

  • Healthy physical growth
  • Improved mental health
  • Better concentration
  • Enhanced learning outcomes
  • Increased creativity
  • Stronger social relationships
  • Higher confidence and resilience
  • Prevention of childhood obesity
  • Lifelong healthy habits

Creating environments where children can play freely supports healthier families, stronger communities, and more inclusive cities.

The Active Play Challenge aims to support organizations developing practical solutions that make these benefits accessible to all children and young people.

What the Challenge Is Looking For

The Active Play Challenge seeks practical, innovative initiatives that increase opportunities for active play for children and young people aged 0 to 25 years.

Projects should make active play:

  • More accessible
  • More inclusive
  • More equitable
  • Easier to integrate into everyday life

Rather than focusing solely on recreational facilities, the challenge encourages applicants to rethink how active play can be incorporated into homes, schools, neighbourhoods, streets, and public spaces.

Challenge Categories

Applicants may submit projects under one of three categories.

1. Play by Design

This category focuses on physical and spatial interventions that incorporate active play into the design of everyday environments.

Examples may include:

  • Streets designed for play
  • Child-friendly public spaces
  • School playground innovations
  • Community parks
  • Playable neighbourhoods
  • Housing developments that encourage movement
  • Interactive public installations
  • Building designs promoting physical activity

The emphasis is on creating environments where children naturally engage in active play during their daily routines.

2. Play in Action

This category supports activities and services that integrate active play into existing spaces and everyday routines.

Examples include:

  • Community programmes
  • School-based initiatives
  • Recreational activities
  • Youth engagement programmes
  • Family participation projects
  • Physical activity campaigns
  • Inclusive sports and play programmes

The objective is to encourage regular movement without necessarily requiring new infrastructure.

3. Play for Early Years

This category specifically supports projects designed for:

  • Children aged 0–5 years
  • Parents
  • Caregivers

The focus is on promoting healthy early childhood development through active play.

Eligible initiatives may include:

  • Early childhood play programmes
  • Parent-child activities
  • Developmental play interventions
  • Community early learning projects
  • Inclusive play environments for toddlers

This category receives additional support from the Van Leer Foundation, reflecting its emphasis on improving outcomes during the critical early years of life.

Who Can Apply?

The Active Play Challenge welcomes applications from:

  • Legally registered organizations worldwide

Organizations should have both:

  • The legal mandate
  • The operational capacity

to lead and implement an initiative that creates opportunities for active play.

The organizers strongly encourage applications from:

  • Youth-led organizations

These are organizations led by individuals aged 18 to 25 years, recognizing the important role young leaders play in designing innovative solutions for healthier communities.

Prize Funding

The Active Play Challenge offers substantial financial support to selected winners.

Winning Initiatives

Up to six winning initiatives will receive funding ranging between:

  • USD $50,000
  • USD $100,000

Funding is intended to help organizations:

  • Refine their ideas
  • Develop implementation plans
  • Deliver their proposed initiatives
  • Scale innovative solutions
  • Increase community impact

Additional Benefits

Beyond financial support, winning organizations will receive:

Technical Guidance

Successful teams will benefit from expert advice throughout the implementation period.

This support may include:

  • Project refinement
  • Technical mentoring
  • Implementation guidance
  • Strategic advice
  • Capacity strengthening

Learning Opportunities

Shortlisted finalists will also receive access to:

  • Selected learning programmes
  • Knowledge-sharing sessions
  • Professional development opportunities

Peer-to-Peer Exchange

Finalists will have opportunities to connect with fellow innovators working on similar initiatives worldwide.

These exchanges promote:

  • Collaboration
  • Shared learning
  • Innovation
  • Best practice dissemination

Why Organizations Should Apply

The Active Play Challenge offers organizations an opportunity to:

  • Access significant implementation funding
  • Improve children’s health and wellbeing
  • Promote healthier cities
  • Develop innovative urban solutions
  • Strengthen organizational capacity
  • Receive international recognition
  • Build partnerships
  • Learn from global experts
  • Join a worldwide network of innovators

For organizations already working in child development, urban planning, education, health, or community development, this challenge provides valuable support for expanding their impact.

Preparing a Strong Application

Applicants should ensure their proposals:

  • Clearly address barriers to active play.
  • Demonstrate innovation.
  • Show measurable community impact.
  • Promote inclusion and equity.
  • Explain how children and young people will benefit.
  • Present realistic implementation plans.
  • Demonstrate organizational capacity to deliver the project.

Projects should be practical, scalable, and sustainable.

How to Apply

Interested organizations should:

  1. Review the eligibility requirements.
  2. Identify the most appropriate challenge category.
  3. Develop a clear and practical proposal.
  4. Submit the application through the official Active Play Challenge application portal.
  5. Review the FAQs and webinar resources if additional guidance is required.

Applicants can also watch the official Q&A webinar recording to better understand:

  • Eligibility requirements
  • Application categories
  • Selection process
  • Frequently asked questions

These resources can help applicants prepare stronger submissions.

APPLY HERE

Application Deadline

The challenge announcement encourages organizations to apply but does not specify an application deadline in the available information. Applicants are therefore advised to visit the official application portal and submit their applications as early as possible once they have prepared all required materials.

Visit HERE to learn more about the Active Play Challenge 2026.

Conclusion

The Active Play Challenge 2026 represents an exciting global opportunity for organizations committed to improving the lives of children and young people through active play. By supporting innovative ideas that transform cities into healthier, more inclusive, and engaging environments, the challenge seeks to inspire practical solutions that can create lasting benefits for communities around the world.

With funding of up to USD $100,000, expert implementation support, learning opportunities, and international networking, the challenge provides a unique platform for organizations to expand their impact while helping children grow, learn, and thrive through everyday active play. Organizations with creative, practical, and scalable ideas are encouraged to prepare strong applications and seize this opportunity to contribute to healthier cities and brighter futures for children worldwide.

Discover more global Competition opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Active Play Challenge 2026: Win Up to USD $100,000 to Create Innovative Active Play Opportunities for Children and Young People Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Youth Climate Mapping Fellowship 2026 for Youth Groups in Lesotho, Eswatini and South Africa | Receive a $400 Climate Action Stipend and Free Digital Mapping Training
item.dc:creator: Evelyn Essuman
item.description:
Climate change continues to affect communities across Africa, creating urgent challenges related to environmental sustainability, disaster preparedness, and resilience. One of the most effective ways to strengthen local climate action is through accurate community mapping, which helps organizations, governments, and humanitarian agencies better understand environmental risks and make informed decisions. To empower young people to […]

The post Youth Climate Mapping Fellowship 2026 for Youth Groups in Lesotho, Eswatini and South Africa | Receive a $400 Climate Action Stipend and Free Digital Mapping Training appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Climate change continues to affect communities across Africa, creating urgent challenges related to environmental sustainability, disaster preparedness, and resilience. One of the most effective ways to strengthen local climate action is through accurate community mapping, which helps organizations, governments, and humanitarian agencies better understand environmental risks and make informed decisions.

To empower young people to become leaders in climate resilience, the Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team (HOT), in partnership with UNICEF YOMA, has launched the Youth Climate Mapping Fellowship. This unique fellowship is designed for youth groups, OpenStreetMap communities, and youth-led organizations based in Lesotho, Eswatini, and South Africa.

The fellowship combines digital mapping, climate education, community engagement, and practical climate action. Selected youth groups will gain access to professional online training, technical support, and financial assistance to implement local climate awareness initiatives within their communities.

If your organization is passionate about climate resilience, community mapping, and youth-led environmental advocacy, this fellowship presents an excellent opportunity to build valuable skills while making a meaningful impact.

About the Youth Climate Mapping Fellowship

The Youth Climate Mapping Fellowship is a capacity-building initiative that equips youth organizations with practical knowledge and digital tools for climate mapping and community resilience.

The program focuses on helping young people learn how geographic data can support climate adaptation, disaster preparedness, environmental planning, and local advocacy efforts.

The fellowship encourages participants to use OpenStreetMap technologies to collect, organize, and analyze location-based information that contributes to stronger community decision-making and climate awareness.

The initiative is delivered by the Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team (HOT), a globally recognized nonprofit organization that uses open mapping technologies to support humanitarian response and sustainable development, in collaboration with UNICEF YOMA, which works to empower young people through learning and social impact opportunities.

Who Can Apply?

Applications are open to youth groups and organizations located in:

  • Lesotho
  • Eswatini
  • South Africa

Eligible organizations include:

  • YouthMappers Chapters
  • OpenStreetMap (OSM) Communities
  • Non-Governmental Organizations (NGOs)
  • Community-Based Organizations (CBOs)
  • Civil Society Organizations (CSOs)
  • Other youth-led organizations or community groups interested in climate action

Applicants must also be capable of mobilizing at least 20 participants who will take part in the fellowship activities.

Fellowship Benefits

Selected youth groups will receive a range of educational and practical benefits designed to strengthen their mapping capacity and climate leadership.

1. Free Self-Paced Online Training

Participants will gain access to two comprehensive online courses at no cost:

Introduction to Open Mapping for Climate Action

This course introduces participants to:

  • Fundamentals of OpenStreetMap
  • Digital mapping concepts
  • Climate mapping techniques
  • Community-based data collection
  • Using geographic information for climate resilience

ChatMap for Field Mapping

Participants will also learn how to:

  • Conduct field mapping activities
  • Collect geographic information
  • Improve community mapping accuracy
  • Generate local climate data
  • Support environmental planning initiatives

Because both courses are self-paced, participants can complete the learning modules according to their own schedules.

2. Technical Support

Throughout the fellowship, participants receive guidance and technical assistance to strengthen their mapping projects and ensure successful implementation of community activities.

Support includes:

  • Digital mapping guidance
  • Climate data collection assistance
  • Community mapping best practices
  • Practical implementation support

3. Climate Action Experience

Learning is combined with real-world implementation.

Participants will organize and conduct a local climate action activity within their communities that promotes:

  • Climate awareness
  • Environmental education
  • Community resilience
  • Public advocacy on climate-related issues

This practical experience allows participants to apply the skills learned during the fellowship while creating measurable local impact.

4. $400 Climate Action Stipend

Each selected youth group will receive a $400 stipend to support the implementation of its local climate action activity.

The funding is intended to assist groups in organizing activities that promote climate education, awareness, and advocacy within their communities.

What Participants Will Learn

The fellowship equips participants with valuable knowledge in several important areas, including:

  • OpenStreetMap fundamentals
  • Community digital mapping
  • Climate resilience
  • Geographic data collection
  • Field mapping techniques
  • Community engagement
  • Climate awareness campaigns
  • Local climate advocacy
  • Environmental data management
  • Digital collaboration tools

Why This Fellowship Matters

Climate action requires reliable local information.

Many communities lack detailed geographic data that can help identify environmental risks, vulnerable populations, infrastructure gaps, and climate-related challenges.

Through this fellowship, young people become active contributors to climate resilience by generating community data that can support:

  • Climate adaptation planning
  • Disaster risk reduction
  • Sustainable development initiatives
  • Community resilience strategies
  • Evidence-based advocacy
  • Local environmental decision-making

The fellowship also strengthens participants’ digital skills, leadership abilities, teamwork, and community engagement experience.

Group Information Required During Application

Applicants will be asked to provide the following information:

  • Email address
  • Group Lead’s name
  • Group Lead’s WhatsApp number
  • Gender
  • Group name
  • Country
  • Town or city where the group is based
  • Brief description of the group
  • Social media or website links (if available)
  • Organization category
  • Confirmation that the group can mobilize at least 20 participants
  • Details of a second contact person, including:
    • Name
    • Phone number
    • WhatsApp number
    • Email address

Organization Categories Accepted

Applicants may identify their organizations under one of the following categories:

  • YouthMappers Chapter
  • OpenStreetMap Community
  • NGO
  • CBO
  • CSO
  • Other

How to Apply

Interested youth groups should complete the official application form by providing accurate information about their organization, leadership team, contact details, and capacity to participate in the fellowship.

Applicants should ensure that all required information is completed before submitting the application.

Visit HERE for more information about Youth Climate Mapping Fellowship 2026.

Applications close Sunday, 26 July 2026.
Questions? esahub-info@hotosm.org

Conclusion

The Youth Climate Mapping Fellowship offers an outstanding opportunity for youth organizations in Lesotho, Eswatini, and South Africa to develop practical digital mapping skills while contributing to meaningful climate action within their communities.

Beyond learning new technologies, participants will become part of a growing movement that uses open geographic data to strengthen climate resilience, improve environmental awareness, and support sustainable local development. With free technical training, mentorship, practical experience, and a $400 climate action stipend, the fellowship empowers young people to transform knowledge into action and create lasting community impact.

Youth groups interested in climate change, digital innovation, and community development are encouraged to submit their applications and take advantage of this opportunity to become climate mapping leaders in their communities.

Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Youth Climate Mapping Fellowship 2026 for Youth Groups in Lesotho, Eswatini and South Africa | Receive a $400 Climate Action Stipend and Free Digital Mapping Training appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Horn Center for Democracy Youth Leadership for Democracy and Solidarity (YLDS) Program 2026: Fully Funded Regional Leadership Opportunity for Young Democratic Leaders in the Horn of Africa
item.dc:creator: Cecilia Twumwaa
item.description:
The Horn Center for Democracy (HCD) has officially opened applications for the first cohort of the Youth Leadership for Democracy and Solidarity (YLDS) Program 2026, a prestigious regional leadership initiative designed to empower emerging youth leaders across the Horn of Africa. The program seeks to nurture a new generation of democratic leaders by providing intensive […]

The post Horn Center for Democracy Youth Leadership for Democracy and Solidarity (YLDS) Program 2026: Fully Funded Regional Leadership Opportunity for Young Democratic Leaders in the Horn of Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Horn Center for Democracy (HCD) has officially opened applications for the first cohort of the Youth Leadership for Democracy and Solidarity (YLDS) Program 2026, a prestigious regional leadership initiative designed to empower emerging youth leaders across the Horn of Africa.

The program seeks to nurture a new generation of democratic leaders by providing intensive leadership training, civic education, mentorship, regional networking opportunities, and collaborative learning experiences.

Young people remain one of the greatest forces for democratic transformation across the Horn of Africa. However, despite representing a significant percentage of the population, many young leaders continue to face barriers to meaningful participation in governance, civic engagement, and policy-making.

To help bridge this gap, the Horn Center for Democracy is implementing the Youth Leadership for Democracy and Solidarity (YLDS) Program as part of its long-term Youth Development Strategy, aimed at strengthening democratic governance, civic participation, regional cooperation, and youth leadership across the Horn of Africa.

Successful applicants will join a select cohort of only 15 emerging youth leaders from across the region.

About the Youth Leadership for Democracy and Solidarity (YLDS) Program

The Youth Leadership for Democracy and Solidarity (YLDS) is a regional leadership development initiative created to strengthen democratic values, encourage civic participation, and build cross-border cooperation among young leaders.

The program combines leadership education with practical democratic engagement, allowing participants to learn directly from democracy practitioners, governance experts, academics, and experienced civic leaders from across Africa.

Rather than being a one-time training event, YLDS serves as the beginning of a long-term regional network of democratic leaders who will continue collaborating through mentorship, partnerships, and alumni engagement.

The program forms part of HCD’s multi-year Youth Development Programme, which focuses on:

  • Digital civic education
  • Leadership development
  • Democratic participation
  • Cross-border cooperation
  • Youth empowerment
  • Regional solidarity
  • Civic innovation

Program Location

The residential leadership program will be held in:

Addis Ababa, Ethiopia

Program Dates

October 11 – October 18, 2026

Participants are expected to attend the entire residential program and all virtual learning activities.

What Participants Will Experience

The selected cohort will benefit from an intensive and interactive learning experience that combines physical and online engagement.

Participants will take part in:

  • A one-week residential leadership training in Addis Ababa
  • Hybrid online learning sessions
  • Interactive workshops
  • Regional dialogue sessions
  • Peer-to-peer learning
  • Collaborative innovation labs
  • Group projects
  • Mentorship opportunities
  • Regional networking
  • Alumni engagement activities

The learning model has been carefully designed to encourage collaboration, knowledge sharing, innovation, and long-term partnerships among young democratic leaders across the Horn of Africa.

Training Themes

Participants will receive practical leadership training covering a broad range of governance and democracy topics, including:

Regional Political Dynamics and Democratization

Participants will examine the political landscape across the Horn of Africa, democratic transitions, governance systems, and regional cooperation.

Leadership and Emotional Intelligence

The training will strengthen participants’ leadership capabilities while developing emotional intelligence, conflict-sensitive leadership, negotiation skills, and responsible decision-making.

Youth and Women’s Agency

Participants will explore the important role of youth and women in advancing democratic governance, civic participation, and inclusive political processes.

Digital Advocacy and Civic Action

The program will equip participants with practical strategies for using digital platforms to promote civic engagement, public participation, advocacy campaigns, and democratic accountability.

Cross-Border Collaboration

Participants will learn how to work collaboratively across national boundaries to address shared governance challenges affecting the Horn of Africa.

Grassroots Democratic Innovation

The program encourages innovative community-based solutions that strengthen democratic participation and social change.

Collaborative Project Design

Participants will work together to design practical initiatives that address democratic challenges within their communities.

Benefits of the Program

Selected participants will have the opportunity to:

  • Gain an in-depth understanding of democratic governance across the Horn of Africa.
  • Develop practical leadership and governance skills.
  • Learn directly from regional democracy experts.
  • Network with young leaders from multiple countries.
  • Exchange experiences and best practices.
  • Build long-lasting professional relationships.
  • Develop collaborative regional projects.
  • Receive mentorship beyond the residential training.
  • Join an expanding alumni network of democratic leaders.
  • Strengthen their ability to influence democratic transformation within their communities.

Who Should Apply?

The Horn Center for Democracy welcomes applications from passionate young leaders committed to advancing democracy, civic participation, and peaceful political engagement.

Applicants should:

  • Be citizens of an IGAD Member State.
  • Be between 18 and 35 years old.
  • Demonstrate leadership potential.
  • Support democratic governance and non-violent political engagement.
  • Have experience in:
    • Civic engagement
    • Youth leadership
    • Community organizing
    • Advocacy
    • Policy engagement
    • Social innovation
    • Political participation
    • Activism
  • Be interested in regional collaboration.
  • Be available for all online and in-person activities.
  • Participate in collaborative project development.
  • Take part in program evaluations.
  • Remain active within the alumni network after completion.

The Horn Center for Democracy promotes diversity, inclusion, and equal opportunity, encouraging applications from qualified young leaders across the region.

Application Requirements

Applicants are required to submit:

  1. A completed online application form.
  2. A current CV (maximum two pages).
  3. A motivation letter explaining:
    • Why they are interested in the program.
    • Their experience in community organizing or development.
    • Their commitment to democratic leadership.
  4. Any additional supporting documents requested in the application.
  5. A 3–5 minute YouTube video demonstrating or explaining their relevant work and experience related to the program. Applicants should upload the video to YouTube and submit only the video URL.

Important Dates

Activity Date
Applications Open July 15, 2026
Application Deadline August 5, 2026
Residential Program October 11–18, 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit all required documents well before the deadline, as late or incomplete applications will not be considered.

Interested applicants are encouraged to visit HERE to learn more about the Youth Leadership for Democracy and Solidarity (YLDS) Program and review the application guidelines.

Why You Should Apply

If you are passionate about strengthening democracy, promoting civic participation, empowering communities, and collaborating with young leaders across borders, the Youth Leadership for Democracy and Solidarity (YLDS) Program offers an outstanding opportunity to develop your leadership capacity while building meaningful regional partnerships.

Beyond acquiring new knowledge, participants will become part of an influential network of emerging democratic leaders working collectively to shape a more inclusive, peaceful, and democratic future for the Horn of Africa.

                                                   Apply HERE 

Conclusion

The Horn Center for Democracy Youth Leadership for Democracy and Solidarity (YLDS) Program 2026 represents a significant opportunity for young leaders committed to democratic governance and regional cooperation. Through intensive leadership development, mentorship, practical learning, and cross-border collaboration, the program equips participants with the knowledge, skills, and professional networks needed to drive meaningful democratic transformation in their communities and across the Horn of Africa. Interested applicants should ensure they complete all application requirements and submit their applications no later than August 5, 2026, to be considered for this highly competitive regional leadership opportunity.
